_legend.engine: xmrig = one-click switch (built, running) · xmrig-merge = needs Tari proxy · cpuminer-opt = yespower family (separate build) · verus = ccminer/verus miner · node = coin's own daemon/miner · boinc = useful-work
_legend.ready: true = address set + engine live, can mine now · false = staged: paste an address and/or install the engine
_legend.address_source: where to get a payout address without running a full wallet (exchange/light-wallet)

coins.name: Monero
coins.note: auto-switches to the most profitable RandomX coin, always pays you in XMR — the smart default

coins.name: Monero
coins.note: classic low-fee Monero pool — pure XMR, no auto-switch

coins.name: Tari
coins.address_source: Tari console wallet (Aurora app or minotari_console_wallet)
coins.note: THE alpha: every Monero hash also mines Tari for free. Needs the merge-mining proxy — see BOOSTERS.md / bin/setup-merge-mine.sh

coins.name: Wownero
coins.note: meme privacy fork, faster RandomX variant — one-click once you paste a WOW address

coins.name: Zephyr
coins.address_source: Zephyr wallet or exchange listing ZEPH
coins.note: algorithmic-stablecoin chain on RandomX — one-click, rx/0 like Monero

coins.name: Salvium
coins.note: privacy + yield chain, rx/0 — one-click

coins.name: ArQmA
coins.note: lightweight RandomX variant — one-click

coins.name: Yada
coins.note: Bitcoin-on-RandomX experiment — one-click

coins.name: Quantum Resistant Ledger
coins.note: post-quantum chain, mines on stock rx/0 — one-click

coins.name: Equilibria
coins.note: oracle/DeFi privacy chain on rx/0 — one-click

coins.name: Xolentum
coins.note: small rx/0 chain, low difficulty — one-click

coins.name: Talecoin
coins.note: community RandomX fork, very low net difficulty = home-CPU friendly — one-click

coins.name: Raptoreum
coins.note: GhostRider is heavier than RandomX → 1 kH is worth more; one-click via xmrig gr

coins.name: Conceal
coins.note: banking-privacy chain on cn/gpu — one-click (CPU works, GPU favored)

coins.name: Verus
coins.note: the CPU mining KING — most ASIC-resistant, best $/CPU. Needs the Verus miner (ccminer-verus), not xmrig — staged

coins.name: DERO
coins.note: homomorphic-encryption chain; AstroBWT now GPU-favored. Needs DERO's miner — staged

coins.name: Sugarchain
coins.note: pure-CPU yespower coin, ASIC/GPU-proof. Needs cpuminer-opt build — staged · cpuminer engine BUILT on arm64 — paste an address to mine

coins.name: Yenten
coins.note: long-running CPU-only coin. · cpuminer engine BUILT on arm64 — paste an address to mine

coins.name: CPUchain
coins.note: literally designed to be CPU-only forever. · cpuminer engine BUILT on arm64 — paste an address to mine

coins.name: Bellscoin
coins.note: revived 2013 CPU coin, meme momentum. · cpuminer engine BUILT on arm64 — paste an address to mine

coins.name: Magi
coins.note: PoW-of-work-with-PoS-cap, CPU-tuned. · cpuminer engine BUILT on arm64 — paste an address to mine

coins.name: Bitcoin
coins.address_source: NiceHash account BTC address (or your own BTC address w/ NiceHash)
coins.note: you don't 'mine BTC' on CPU — you sell RandomX hashpower to NiceHash and get paid in BTC. One-click via xmrig once you set a NiceHash BTC address

coins.name: Gridcoin
coins.note: NOT PoW → the only thing the VPS may run per Contabo/OVH ToS. Both VPS attached to grcpool; enable projects on grcpool.com Hosts page to start crunching
